title: Project Overview
description: Start with the README and package.json to understand the project's purpose as a Voxel Blade damage calculator, its tech stack (Svelte 5 + Vite + TypeScript), and development workflow.

title: Application Entry Point
description: See how the frontend boots: index.html loads main.ts, which imports global styles and mounts the root App.svelte component.

title: UI Component Architecture
description: Explore the reusable UI primitives (Badge, Button, Card, Panel) and layout components (AppHeader, Modal) that form the foundation of the interface.

title: Game Data Definitions
description: Examine the game data modules that define weapon arts, perk damage formulas, base weapon damage, and other RPG mechanics used throughout the calculator.

title: Calculation Engine
description: Understand the core damage calculation pipeline: engine.ts re-exports all calculation modules, while defense.ts handles armor and damage reduction logic.

title: Constants & Configuration
description: Review the centralized constants that drive the engine: damage types, game mechanics (HP, level caps), perk multipliers, and storage keys.

title: State Management & Utilities
description: Learn how application state is managed (toast notifications, build saves) and how utility functions like enchantment tooltips are generated.

title: Damage Analysis Pipeline
description: Follow the main damage calculation flow: DamageAnalyzer assembles buffs and computes full damage, BaseDamageCalc displays per-hit breakdowns, and BuffList shows active effects.
